title: Google Calendar Automations
excerpt:

posts.author: Dende (Dende Photos)
posts.cookedHtml:
I currently have an automation set that transfers an item from a lead board to a project board. Once in the project board there is an automation to create a calendar event based on specific date columns.

However whenever the automation is triggered from the leads board to the project board, it creates 2 calendar invites instead of 1.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Dende,

Just to clarify, when the item is moved from the leads board to the project board, it’s creating two events in your google calendar or two items in your project boards? Would you be able to send a screenshot of the automations / integrations you have set up in both boards, incase there is a double trigger occurring?

posts.author: Dende (Dende Photos)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BiancaT

The automation creates only 1 item in the board, but 2 events on my google calendar. I just did a test and it seems like its not occurring anymore.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad to hear this seemed to have resolved itself! That said, be sure to reach out to support@monday.com if you run into this issue again so that our technical specialists can investigate

posts.author: jparty (Javier Party)
posts.cookedHtml:
Maybe it is not related but have you noticed that choosing a time and a date triggers twice?

BUG: Selecting Date & Time triggers twice Special workflows & use cases
By default the date column has the date set to the current date and the time set to 9:00. If you choose a date: it closes the pop-up without letting you choose the time and it triggers your workflow once. It sends the current date and the time set to 9:00. If you choose a time first: it does not close the pop-up, but it does trigger your workflow once already, sending the current date and the time set to whatever you chose. If you then choose the right date, it triggers your workflow again, no…
